entities.description: A fourteenth-century vellum octavo composite. Hanna identifies nine Latin leaves formerly at the front of part I as now separately preserved as Bodleian MS Lat. th. e. 32. Directly inspected historical sources report 220 or 221 leaves.

reviewNotes: The raw U-M package, its raw XML, the U-M TIFF facsimile, the repository XML, and the IA metadata record are distinct layers even when one ZIP contains several of them.
Bülbring's first-thirty-seven-Psalms intermediary is explicit. Do not simplify the complete primary path to direct Bülbring autopsy of all London text.
Dublin is a supporting collation source for Bülbring, not the main-text witness.
The TCD CAPTCHA response is modeled as a response entity with target presence absent; its route is only possible unknown-extent image access and never a facsimile local copy.

All 28 cached files have their own entity, access/local-copy record, rights record, and evidence record; the U-M package's one fixed local copy is additionally projected to its contained encoding and TIFF-facsimile access routes without duplicating unique-byte totals.
Every relation is classified exactly once as primary or supporting.

Drafted only from the independently validated frozen baa8159 packet (freeze-file SHA-256 6be0bdc5a9ca6acbbe2792c95349b11108079918234885733cdd4cc8bf4c0a45; packet aggregate f60e33e0ab4577fa29f18b884b5c05a7ba889665a1392595cccf6d14c691279e).
The exact Magdalena Charzyńska-Wójcik identity is retained; no obsolete researcher identity is introduced.
TCD MS 69 has a physical-witness entity, catalog routes, and a conservative possible-image enquiry route only. The challenge HTML is never modeled as a facsimile or target-bearing local copy.

lineage.summary: The repository artifact is a pinned, structurally normalized copy of U-M BAA8159.0001.001. U-M OCR was made from a complete 220-image facsimile of Bülbring's 1891 edition. Bülbring printed the English text of BL Add MS 17376, using Matthias Konrath's careful working transcript for Psalms 1-37 and his own London transcription thereafter, and collated TCD MS 69 throughout. Four complete English witnesses now are known. A later BnF French witness represents the intermediary French tradition but is not the translator's physical exemplar. Complete raw U-M, Princeton, and Gallica bundles and complete Bülbring and Charzyńska PDFs are cached; TCD's viewer was access-challenged, BL has a paid photography route, and Pepys 2498 is absent from the current official selection.

catalogSummary.date: First half of the fourteenth century (cautiously 1300–1350)
catalogSummary.editor: Karl Daniel Bülbring (1891 immediate source edition)
catalogSummary.region: England; no narrower composition place securely established
catalogSummary.title: Middle English Glossed Prose Psalter

catalogingSubject.label: Middle English Glossed Prose Psalter
catalogingSubject.scope_note: One anonymous Middle English prose translation comprising 150 Psalms, eleven canticles, and the Athanasian Creed. It is not the whole Bülbring volume, any whole manuscript, the Latin glossed source, or the later French representative.

contentParts.label: Psalm 1
notes: XML descendant counts: P=8, PB=1, MILESTONE=2, NOTE1=11.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 1. Incipit: Blesced be þe man, þat ȝede nouȝt in þe counseil of wicked, ne stode nouȝt in þe waie of sinȝeres, ne sat nauȝt in fals iugement. Explicit: For oure Lord knew þe waie of þe ryȝtful, and þe waye of synners schal perissen.

contentParts.label: Psalm 2
notes: XML descendant counts: P=13, PB=1, MILESTONE=3, NOTE1=15.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 2. Incipit: Whi douteþ hij hem of þe lawe, þe folk wyþ-outen lawe, & folk þoȝt idel þynges? Explicit: Whan he be styred in hys short ire, blisced ben hij, þat afien in hym.

contentParts.label: Psalm 3
notes: XML descendant counts: P=8, MILESTONE=1, NOTE1=10.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 3. Incipit: Lord, why ben hij multiplied þat trublen me? mani arisen aȝeins me. Explicit: Helþe ys of oure Lord, and þy blisseing, Lord, hys on þy folk.

contentParts.label: Psalm 4
notes: XML descendant counts: P=10, PB=1, MILESTONE=2, NOTE1=15.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 4. Incipit: As ich cleped, God of my ryȝt herd me; þou, Lord, forbare me in my tribulacioun. Explicit: For þou, Lord, haþ on-liche stablist me in hope.

contentParts.label: Psalm 5
notes: XML descendant counts: P=15, PB=1, MILESTONE=2, NOTE1=16.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 5. Incipit: Lord, take myn wordes wyþ þyne eren; vnderstonde my crye. Explicit: Lord, þou hast crouned us as wyþ þe shelde of þy gode wylle.

contentParts.label: Psalm 6
notes: XML descendant counts: P=10, PB=1, MILESTONE=2, NOTE1=16.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 6. Incipit: Lord, ne repruce me nouȝt in þy vengeaunce; ne reproue me nouȝt in þyn yre. Explicit: Wax alle myn enemys asshamed, and ben hij greteliche trubled; ben hij conuerted, & shame hij ful swyftlich.

contentParts.label: Psalm 7
notes: XML descendant counts: P=18, PB=1, MILESTONE=3, NOTE1=25.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 7. Incipit: Lord, my God, ich hoped in þe; make me saufe of alle þat pursuen me, & deliuer me fram alle yuel; Explicit: Ich shal shryue to our Lord after hys ryȝtfulnesse, and synge to þe name of þe heȝest Lord.

contentParts.label: Psalm 8
notes: XML descendant counts: P=9, PB=1, MILESTONE=1, NOTE1=11.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 8. Incipit: Ha þou, Lord, our Lord, ful wonderful hys þy name in al þerþe. Explicit: Ha Lord, our Lord, ful wonderful ys þy name in alle erþe.

contentParts.label: Psalm 9
notes: XML descendant counts: P=41, PB=3, MILESTONE=7, NOTE1=66, NOTE2=1.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 9. Incipit: Ich shal shryue to þe, Lord, in alle myn hert; ich shal tellen al þyn wonders. Explicit: To iuge þe moderles and þe meke, þat man sett nouȝt to herien hem vp þerþe.

contentParts.label: Psalm 10
notes: XML descendant counts: P=8, PB=1, MILESTONE=1, NOTE1=9.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 10. Incipit: Ich affie me in our Lord; hou saie ȝe wicked to mi soule, Wende þou in-to heuen as a sparwe? Explicit: For our Lord ys riȝtful, ande he loueþ riȝtfulnes; hys semblaun saiȝ euennis.

contentParts.label: Psalm 11
notes: XML descendant counts: P=9, MILESTONE=2, NOTE1=13.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 11. Incipit: Ha Lord, make me sauf, for þe holi failed in parfit holynes; for soþnes ben litteled fram mennes sones. Explicit: Þe wicked ȝeden abouten, þou multiplidest mennes sones efter þyn eȝenes.

contentParts.label: Psalm 12
notes: XML descendant counts: P=6, PB=1, MILESTONE=1, NOTE1=9.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 12. Incipit: Ha Sir, hou longe forȝetestou me on ende? hou longe turnestou þy face fram me? Explicit: Min hert shal ioien in þyn helþe; ich shal singe to our Lord, þat ȝaf to me godes, and y shal synge to þe name of our Lord alderȝeste (!).

contentParts.label: Psalm 13
notes: XML descendant counts: P=11, PB=1, MILESTONE=1, NOTE1=17.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 13. Incipit: Þe vnwyse seid in his hert, It nys God. Explicit: Who shal ȝyf fram þe heuen helþe to Israel? whan our Lord haþ turned oway þe chaytifnesse of hijs folk, þe kynreden of Iakob shal gladen, and þe folk of Israel shal ioyen.

contentParts.label: Psalm 14
notes: XML descendant counts: P=7, MILESTONE=2, NOTE1=11.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 14. Incipit: Lord, who shal wonen in þy tabernacle, oþer who shal resten in þyn holy hill? Explicit: He þat doþ þes þynges, ne shal nouȝt be stired wyþ-outen ende.

contentParts.label: Psalm 15
notes: XML descendant counts: P=11, PB=1, MILESTONE=1, NOTE1=16.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 15. Incipit: Kepe me, Lord, for ich hoped in þe; ich seide to our Lord, Þou art my God, for þou ne hast no nede of myn godes. Explicit: Þou madest knowen to me þe waies of lyf, þou shalt fulfillen me of ioie wyþ [þy] face; delitynges ben in þy riȝt honde vnto þe ende.

contentParts.label: Psalm 16
notes: XML descendant counts: P=17, PB=1, MILESTONE=3, NOTE1=19.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 16. Incipit: Here, Lord, my riȝt; vnderstonde my praier. Explicit: And y shal apperen in ryȝt to þy siȝt; y shal be fild, whan þy glorie haþ shewed.

contentParts.label: Psalm 17
notes: XML descendant counts: P=53, PB=4, MILESTONE=8, NOTE1=64.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 17. Incipit: Ha Lord, y shal loue þe, my strengþe, my fasteninge, my refut, and my deliuerer of iuel. Explicit: Heriand þe helþes of his kynge and doand mercy to hys Dauid anoit wyþ creme and hys sede vnto þe world.

contentParts.label: Psalm 18
notes: XML descendant counts: P=16, PB=1, MILESTONE=3, NOTE1=20, NOTE2=1.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 18. Incipit: Þe heuens tellen þe glorie of God, and þe firmament telleþ þe werkes of his hondes. Explicit: Lord, þou art myn helper and myn oȝainbyger.

contentParts.label: Psalm 19
notes: XML descendant counts: P=10, PB=1, MILESTONE=1, NOTE1=8.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 19. Incipit: Ha þou my soule, her our Lord þe in þe day of þy tribulacioun; þe name of þe God Jacob defende þe fram iuel. Explicit: Lord, make þou þe kyng sauf, and her us in þe daie þat we haue cleped þe.

contentParts.label: Psalm 20
notes: XML descendant counts: P=13, PB=1, MILESTONE=3, NOTE1=14.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 20. Incipit: Lord, þe kyng shal gladen in þy vertu, and he shal greteleche ioyen vp þyn helþe. Explicit: Be þou, Lord, an-heȝed in þy vertu; we shul syngen & psalmen þy vertuȝ.

contentParts.label: Psalm 21
notes: XML descendant counts: P=33, PB=2, MILESTONE=5, NOTE1=40.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 21. Incipit: Ha God, my God, loke in me; whi for-soke þou me? þe wordes of min trespases ben fer fram myn helþe. Explicit: Þe kynde þat hys to comen shal be shewed to our Lord, and þe heuens shul shewe hys riȝtfulnes to þe folk þat shal ben borne, which our Lord made.

contentParts.label: Psalm 22
notes: XML descendant counts: P=9, PB=1, MILESTONE=1, NOTE1=10.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 22. Incipit: Our Lord gouerneþ me, and noþyng shal defailen to me; in þe stede of pasture he sett me þer. Explicit: And þat ich wonne in þe hous of our Lord in lengþe of daies.

contentParts.label: Psalm 23
notes: XML descendant counts: P=10, PB=1, MILESTONE=2, NOTE1=13.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 23. Incipit: Þe erþe is our Lordes and his plente, þe world and ichon þat woneþ þer-inne. Explicit: Which is he, þat kynge of glorie? Þe Lord of uertu, he his kynge of glorie.

contentParts.label: Psalm 24
notes: XML descendant counts: P=23, PB=1, MILESTONE=3, NOTE1=23.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 24. Incipit: Lord, ich lefted my soule to þe: þou art my God; ich affied me in þe, ich ne shal nouȝt shame. Explicit: Delyuer þou, God, þe folk of Israel of alle her tribulacions.

contentParts.label: Psalm 25
notes: XML descendant counts: P=12, PB=1, MILESTONE=1, NOTE1=13.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 25. Incipit: Iuge me, Lord, for ich entred in myn innocens; and ich hoped in our Lord, ne shal nouȝt be made syke. Explicit: My fote stode in stede dresced; Lord, ich shal blisse þe in chirches.

contentParts.label: Psalm 26
notes: XML descendant counts: P=20, PB=1, MILESTONE=3, NOTE1=21, NOTE2=1.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 26. Incipit: Our Lord, which ich shal douten, is my liȝtyng and my helpe. Explicit: Abyde our Lord, and do manneleche; and þyn hert be conforted, and kepe wyþ þe our Lord.

contentParts.label: Psalm 27
notes: XML descendant counts: P=12, PB=1, MILESTONE=2, NOTE1=10.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 27. Incipit: Lord, my God, y shal crye to þe; ne stylle nouȝt fro me, ne let nouȝt fro me; and y shal be liche to þe descendand in þe diche. Explicit: Make, Lord, þy folk sauf, and blisce þyn heritage, and gouerne hem, [& anheȝe hem] vnto wyþouten ende.

contentParts.label: Psalm 28
notes: XML descendant counts: P=10, PB=1, MILESTONE=2, NOTE1=14, NOTE2=2.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 28. Incipit: Ha ȝe Goddes sones, bringeþ to our Lord, bryngeþ to our Lord sacrifice of weþers. Explicit: Our Lord shal heue vertu to his folk, and our Lord shal bliscen his folkes in pes.

contentParts.label: Psalm 29
notes: XML descendant counts: P=15, PB=1, MILESTONE=2, NOTE1=17.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 29. Incipit: Y shal heȝe þe Lord, for þou herdest me, and madest nouȝt myn enemys on brede vp me. Explicit: Þat mi glorie synge to þe, and y ne be nouȝt prikked; Lord, my God, y shal shryue to þe wyþ-outen ende.

contentParts.label: Psalm 30
notes: XML descendant counts: P=31, PB=2, MILESTONE=5, NOTE1=29.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 30. Incipit: Ich hoped in þe, Lord; y ne shal nouȝt be counfounded wyþ-outen ende for þy riȝtfulnes; deliure me fram iuel. Explicit: Doþ manneliche, and beþ ȝour hert conforted, ȝe alle þat hopen in our Lord.

contentParts.label: Psalm 31
notes: XML descendant counts: P=14, PB=1, MILESTONE=1, NOTE1=23.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 31. Incipit: Ben hij blysced of wich þe wickednesses ben for-ȝeuen and of which þe synnes ben hed. Explicit: Gladeþ, ȝe ryȝtful in our Lord, and beþ ioyful and glorieþ, ȝe alle riȝtful of hert.

contentParts.label: Psalm 32
notes: XML descendant counts: P=22, PB=1, MILESTONE=4, NOTE1=20, NOTE2=1.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 32. Incipit: Gladeþ, ȝe ryȝtful in our Lord; heryynge bicomeþ to þe ryȝtful. Explicit: Be, Lord, þy merci made up us as we hoped in þe.

contentParts.label: Psalm 33
notes: XML descendant counts: P=22, PB=2, MILESTONE=3, NOTE1=25.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 33. Incipit: Y shal bliscen our Lord in al time; be his heryyng euermore in my mouþe. Explicit: Our Lord shal bigen þe soules of his seruauntȝ; and alle þat hopen in hym ne shul nouȝt trespassen.

contentParts.label: Psalm 34
notes: XML descendant counts: P=32, PB=2, MILESTONE=5, NOTE1=37.
contentParts.summary: Psalm 34. Incipit: Iuge, Lord, þe anoiand me; fiȝt oȝain þe fitand wiþ me. Explicit: And my tunge shal þenchen þy riȝtfulnes and þy heryynge ich day.

contentParts.label: Psalm 35
notes: XML descendant counts: P=13, PB=1, MILESTONE=1, NOTE1=18.

summaries.value: Anonymous Middle English prose translation, probably composed in the first half of the fourteenth century, of a glossed Gallican Psalter and appended canticles and Athanasian Creed. Linguistic evidence indicates that the English rendering was made at least partly through a French intermediary. It survives complete in four manuscripts: BL Add MS 17376, TCD MS 69, Princeton Scheide MS 143, and Cambridge, Magdalene College, Pepys MS 2498. The work is not securely the earliest complete Middle English prose Psalter, is not by William of Shoreham, and cannot be localized to London merely because the surviving witnesses' scribal language has been localized there.

The cataloged work is the complete 162-part Middle English prose translation: 150 Psalms, eleven canticles, and the Athanasian Creed. Bülbring’s preliminary matter, errata, residual page scaffolding, Latin source layer, manuscript non-target contents, and later scholarship are outside the intellectual-work boundary.

All four complete English witnesses transmit Latin verse and glosses with the English translation, but the repository target serializes Bülbring’s English edition. The French mediation is supported at least in part; BnF Français 6260 is collateral and not the translator’s physical exemplar.
